{{Chess Opening Theory/Position|Double Danish Gambit| |rd|nd|bd|qd|kd|bd|nd|rd|pd|pd|pd|pd| |pd|pd|pd| | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | |bl| |pl| | | | | |pd| | | | | |pl|pl| | | |pl|pl|pl|rl|nl|bl|ql|kl| |nl|rl|| }} =Danish Gambit Accepted - Double Danish= ==4. Bc4== 4. Bc4 gambits a second pawn. It develops the light-squared bishop to a place attacking Black's weakest square f7. Black almost always accepts the second pawn with '''[[/4...cxb2|4...cxb2]]''', rather than offering a transposition to the Göring Gambit with '''[[/4...Nc6|4...Nc6]]'''. In one rare line, Black advances the pawn to c2, not wanting to let White develop the dark squared bishop onto the long diagonal. ==Theory table== '''1. e4 e5 2. d4 exd4 3. c3 dxc3 4. Bc4'''
4 5 6
